title: GTA 6 Pre-Order Date, Price & Bonuses | 2026 Guide
subtitle: Take-Two signals a $70-$80 price window, pre-orders are expected well before the November 19 launch, and multiple editions are anticipated
thumbnail_alt: Gaming controller with neon lights representing GTA 6 pre-order guide
content_html:
Quick Answer

GTA 6 pre-orders have not officially opened as of May 2026. Take-Two has confirmed a November 19, 2026 release date on PS5 and Xbox Series X|S. The price is expected to be between $70 and $80. Pre-orders will likely open 60-90 days before launch, placing the window at August-September 2026.

Key Takeaways

  • ✓Release date confirmed: November 19, 2026 on PS5 and Xbox Series X|S
  • ✓Price range: $70-$80, with Take-Two earnings signaling the higher end
  • ✓Pre-order window expected: August-September 2026, based on Rockstar's historical pattern
  • ✓PC release: Not announced for launch day. Take-Two has not committed to a PC date
  • ⚠No pre-order bonuses announced yet as of this writing. Expect Rockstar to reveal editions 8-12 weeks before launch

GTA 6 Release Date | November 19, 2026 on Console

Rockstar Games confirmed GTA 6's release date as November 19, 2026, targeting PS5 and Xbox Series X|S exclusively at launch. The date was first locked publicly during Take-Two Interactive's fiscal year 2026 earnings call, where CEO Strauss Zelnick characterized the launch as one of the most significant entertainment events in the company's history.

November 19 puts GTA 6 in a holiday window, arriving the week before Thanksgiving in the US and giving the title a full holiday retail cycle. Take-Two has described its net bookings outlook as $6.7 billion for fiscal 2027, with GTA 6 as the primary driver. The Take-Two earnings breakdown published by OzoneNews goes deeper on the financial context.

Confirmed Launch Details

Release date November 19, 2026
Platforms PS5 and Xbox Series X|S
PC release Not announced — expected 12-18 months after console
Developer Rockstar Games
Publisher Take-Two Interactive

GTA 6 Price | $70, $80, or Higher?

The price of GTA 6 has not been officially set as of May 2026, but Take-Two's public statements point strongly toward a $79.99 standard edition for console, with potential for higher-priced deluxe editions. Here is the evidence:

During Take-Two's May 2026 earnings call, CEO Strauss Zelnick declined to confirm a specific price but referenced the game's "extraordinary production value" and stated that Take-Two prices its titles "appropriately to reflect consumer value." That language mirrors what Zelnick said before GTA V's 2013 launch, which shipped at the then-standard $59.99 and later re-released at $69.99 for next-gen consoles.

The broader industry context: $69.99 is the current PS5 and Xbox standard for major releases. Sony and Microsoft first-party titles have held at $69.99 since 2020. However, GTA 6 is not a typical release. DFC Intelligence analyst David Cole projected in March 2026 that Take-Two could justify an $80 launch price based on the game's scope and the lack of a competitive release in the same window.

What Analysts Are Saying

DFC Intelligence's March 2026 forecast projected GTA 6 could clear $1 billion in pre-orders alone at an $80 price point, citing the 12-year wait since GTA V and a largely uncontested holiday window. At $70, that number rises to an estimated $1.2B in pre-orders.

GTA 6 Pre-Order Date | When Will Pre-Orders Open?

Rockstar Games and Take-Two have not announced a pre-order date. Based on Rockstar's historical pattern and the November 19 launch window, here is the most likely timeline:

  • GTA V (2013): Pre-orders opened approximately 4 months before launch
  • Red Dead Redemption 2 (2018): Pre-orders opened approximately 3 months before launch
  • GTA V Next-Gen (2022): Pre-orders opened 6 weeks before launch

For GTA 6, the most likely pre-order window is August or September 2026, coinciding with what is expected to be a major marketing push. Rockstar typically releases a second trailer 60-90 days before launch. The GTA 6 Trailer 2 breakdown on OzoneNews covers the confirmed details from that announcement.

Best practice: do not pre-order from third-party scalpers or gray market sites. Pre-orders will be available through PlayStation Store, Xbox Store, and major retailers including Amazon, GameStop, Best Buy, and Walmart once officially announced.

GTA 6 Editions | Standard, Deluxe, and Collector

No editions have been officially announced as of May 2026. However, based on Rockstar's pattern with Red Dead Redemption 2 and GTA V, the following edition structure is likely:

Anticipated Edition Structure (Not Official)

Edition Expected Price Likely Inclusions
Standard Edition $70-$80 Base game only
Deluxe / Special Edition $90-$100 Base game + in-game currency + cosmetic items + early access
Collector's Edition $150-$250 Physical collectibles, steelbook, art book, exclusive in-game content

This table reflects analyst expectations and historical Rockstar patterns. No official editions have been confirmed.

GTA 6 Pre-Order Bonuses | What to Expect

No pre-order bonuses have been announced. Rockstar has historically offered in-game currency (Shark Cards equivalent), cosmetic items, or exclusive vehicles as pre-order incentives. For GTA Online's successor — which is confirmed to launch alongside GTA 6 — pre-order bonuses tied to the online mode are especially likely.

Retailer-specific bonuses (a longstanding industry practice) may also be offered. Best Buy, GameStop, and Amazon have historically provided exclusive in-game items when players pre-order through their platforms.

Our recommendation: Wait for official Rockstar confirmation before pre-ordering. The November 19 date is locked, the game has no competitive pressure from other releases, and there is no meaningful advantage to pre-ordering early other than securing physical copies at retailers with limited stock.

GTA 6 on PC | No Launch Date Yet

GTA 6 will not launch on PC on November 19, 2026. Take-Two has not announced a PC release date. Based on Rockstar's pattern, a PC release is likely 12-18 months after the console launch, placing it in late 2027 or early 2028.

GTA V launched on PS3 and Xbox 360 in September 2013 and did not arrive on PC until April 2015, a gap of 19 months. Red Dead Redemption 2 launched on console in October 2018 and on PC in November 2019, a 13-month gap. GTA 6 will almost certainly follow a similar pattern.

For the latest updates on GTA 6's development and any new announcements, bookmark the OzoneNews GTA 6 hub and the video games news hub.
